While talking, a previous official came and said, "You have been asked to return for a long time, why are you still lingering here?" I followed and left the office. In front, there was a large pond, surrounded by walls. In the middle of the pond was a path made of connected stone slabs, which made a hollow sound when stepped on. Suddenly, I fell into the water, which was like a whirlpool, spinning very fast, and my heart was very anxious and distressed. Suddenly, I saw thousands of lotus lamps on the shore, flickering and shining, moving back and forth irregularly. It moved very fast, and the lamps also gradually moved away, suddenly grounding, and I could see nothing. Looking at it, it was the shore of the Jade Belt River in the capital, the moonlight had fallen to the west, and the drum of the watchtower had struck five times. We helped each other onto the shore, sent Uncle Zhou out of the north gate, and already returned west to the house. Suddenly, I woke up, lying on the bed, looking at the moonlight, listening to the drum beats, everything was like a dream. From then on, the illness was cured.

In the region of Saijiang, there were two scholars who were close friends. Scholar A was older and had a serious demeanor. B's wife called Scholar A "Uncle" and treated him as part of the family. Soon after, B's wife passed away, and he remarried a young and beautiful woman. Scholar A, feeling uncomfortable, did not visit, and they lost contact over time. One day, during a late afternoon rain, they took shelter in a tea pavilion, about two li from B's house. Suddenly, B's first wife appeared, and Scholar A was moved and changed color. B's first wife said, "Uncle, do not be afraid. I have a request of Uncle. My husband's second wife is diligent in household affairs and good at raising my children. If the hanging ghost knows, it will make her hang herself. If this person dies, our family will be ruined. Please go and save my husband." Scholar A said, "I am not a sorcerer or witch, how can I drive away ghosts? You in the underworld, can you not prevent this?" B's first wife said, "It is a wicked and violent spirit, how dare I fight it? Uncle must go." Scholar A, reluctantly, followed her.

As they reached the gate, they found it already closed. 乙的妻子 (Yi's wife) had already entered through a small gap on the side and opened the door. They did not know when the lamp had been lit. She moved a chair to the courtyard and said to 甲 (Jia), "Sit here, Brother. If a beautiful woman comes asking for passage, it will be a hanged ghost. Sit firmly and do not move; she will not dare to come forward. I will watch from behind the seat." A short while later, indeed, a woman appeared, holding a red handkerchief and speaking softly with a smile. "I have something to do and wish to pass through, why don't you step back a bit?" Jia did not respond, and the woman stepped back. Yi's wife said, "She will come back, and when she does, her demeanor will be very unpleasant. Brother, do not be afraid." Soon after, the woman returned and said, "Why don't you avoid me?" Jia still did not pay attention. Suddenly, the ghost appeared with disheveled hair and bloody hands, rushing to Jia's front. Jia shouted loudly at the ghost, and the ghost disappeared. Yi's wife said, "What a pity! Brother, do not call out. Instead, use your left hand's two fingers to write the character '魄' (soul), point it into the ground. Once she enters, she cannot come out. Although she is temporarily gone, she will secretly go to my home. Brother, you can quickly knock on my husband's bedroom door."

Person A followed the instructions, and Person B recognized his voice from a dream. Person B said, "Why have you come so late at night, brother?" Person A replied, "Do not ask me, but rather, ask about the well-being of your wife." Person B searched around the bed but could not find anything, so he quickly opened the door and called for Person A to enter. When lit, it was found hanging behind the bed. They untied the noose, poured hot water over the person, and gradually revived them. Person B asked his wife, "Why did you seek death?" His wife said, "I did not know at first, but there was a woman who vaguely invited me to the garden. After playing for a short while, she saw something that seemed like a round window and instructed me to look through it. When my head went through the window, I could not get out." Person A then explained what he had experienced, but there was no trace of Person B's former wife. A geographer named Lu Zaitian from Jiangxi Province was friends with Person A and spoke about the matter. ○ A mute named Cai, born in Changzhou, could not speak. His surname was Cai, but his given name is unknown. He lived in the north of the city in Qing Shan Zhai and begged for a living due to poverty. People called him "Cai the Mute." The mute had no other skills, and among the beggars, none were better than him. However, only the Taoist Xu treated him kindly. After some time, the Taoist Xu died in Zhu Village with severe injuries. The Xu family accused Zhu of the crime and, through torture, formed a case, sentencing him to death. Some said, "Zhu indeed killed him, and his crime is truly deserving of punishment." Others said, "There might be a miscarriage of justice." However, no one knew the exact details.

One day, Cai the Mute arrived at Zhu Village. The villagers said, "The mute has come; let us feed him." Suddenly, Cai the Mute opened his eyes wide and spoke loudly, "I have come to clear the wrong for the Zhu family; I have no time to eat." The old and young villagers were startled. At that time, the Zhu family had lost all their property due to a case involving Xu the Taoist, and they had no plan in sight. They told Cai the Mute, "This concerns human life; you must not joke." Cai the Mute replied, "When I reach the authorities, I will clarify it myself." Thus, the Zhu family and hundreds of neighbors pulled Cai the Mute into the city. Governor Li Gong was just sitting in the main hall when he interrogated Cai the Mute. Cai the Mute said, "The killer is Xu Yu Gong; what does he have to do with Zhu?" He detailed the facts clearly, and they immediately summoned Xu Yu Gong. Xu Yu Gong was just gambling with his friends under a gourd pavilion to avoid the heat. When summoned, he confessed after one interrogation, and Zhu was released from prison. Initially, Xu Yu Gong had a dispute with Zhu over a guest and failed to resolve it, so he devised a plan to lure Xu the Taoist to a secluded place, beat him to death, and carried the body to Zhu's door. The matter was very secret, but it uniquely did not avoid Cai the Mute, because he was born mute and could not speak. Zhu was grateful for the gift of life and went to the beggars to thank them. The beggars said, "Ah! The mute is dead!" It was on the day Zhu was released from prison.

```json { "source": "○珠泾纪事嘉兴珠泾地濒湖。有童年十三岁，跨牛背，缰绳拴于腰，饮牛于湖。牛入水渐深，没及童足。久许，牛忽惊走，童颠堕水。岸上人恍见有物排浪吞童。牛奔上岸，绳尾拽起一鲇鱼，形如小舟。群哗然。始知牛初为鱼所啮，负痛而奔；奔太速，童遂堕；而童与牛绳相系，鱼虽饵童，而绳不得脱，因为牛曳出，如鱼人之钓者。众操刀斫鱼，冀童尚可救。及童出，气已绝，而衣服发肤毫无所损。脔鱼肉称之，得三百八十余斤。封君朱绪三自吴门归述其事，云乾隆五十四年七月十一日。", "translation": "Records of the Pearl Stream in Jiaxing: The Pearl Stream is located near a lake. There was a thirteen-year-old boy who rode on the back of a cow, with the reins tied around his waist, and let the cow drink water from the lake. The cow entered the water gradually deeper, until the water reached up to the boy's feet. After some time, the cow suddenly became frightened and ran away, causing the boy to fall headfirst into the water. People on the shore suddenly saw something pushing through the waves and swallowing the boy. The cow ran ashore, dragging a large catfish out of the water with the rope, the fish resembling a small boat. The crowd reacted with commotion. It was then understood that the cow was first bitten by the fish and ran away in pain; the cow ran too fast, causing the boy to fall; although the fish tried to eat the boy, the rope prevented it from escaping, and the fish was dragged out by the cow, just like a fisherman's catch. The crowd took out knives to cut the fish, hoping the boy could still be saved. When the boy was pulled out, he was already dead, but his clothes and body were undamaged. The fish was cut into pieces and weighed, totaling over 380 pounds. Lord Zhu Xusan, returning from Wu Gate, recounted the event, saying it happened on July 11th, the 54th year of the Qianlong era." } ```

The Sister of Ye Shizhao The sister of Ye Shizhao, a high-ranking official, married into the Zhang family but was widowed after less than forty days of marriage. She had no children and returned to her mother's home to observe widowhood, while Ye Shizhao petitioned the court for commendation. In the year 1779 during the reign of Emperor Qianlong, the sister was seventy-two years old. One day in autumn, while she was strolling in the garden, a sudden cold wind like an arrow shot directly at her heart. She lay in bed, and medical treatment was ineffective, but her appetite suddenly increased. She usually kept a vegetarian diet, but after the illness, she craved meat and fish and could eat as much as several people. She spoke to nothing all day, and her hands made gestures of resistance and pushing away. There were occasional bruises on her cheeks and chin, and she cried out all night. Her maidservants could not sleep, but only when her brother-in-law was present, she would sleep for a short while. Like this for several months, doctors could not diagnose her illness. Ye Shizhao took advantage of the time when her sister's spirit was slightly clearer and asked her who she was speaking to all day and where the pain and itching were that made her cry out incessantly. The sister did not answer at first, but when he insisted, she sighed deeply and said, "It is the karma from my past life. On that day, while I was strolling in the garden, a sudden cold wind blew, and I was terrified. I hurried back to the room and saw a short, ugly woman with a pockmarked face, wearing a white cotton dress, all patched up, carrying two small boys who were also ugly and ragged. The woman called me 'husband,' and the children called me 'father.' In my past life, I was a man from Jiangxi, surnamed Gu, wealthy, with the woman as my wife and the two boys as my sons. I disliked the woman's ugliness and poisoned her, along with the two sons, and then married two beautiful women, dying of natural causes. The woman's grievance remained unresolved for a century, and she has been seeking me for revenge."

During the previous year, I met Zhang Dexin. It turned out that in a past life, Zhang Dexin and I had a familial connection. He told me that I was here and led me to the garden. However, since the room had a spirit-medium altar, I could not enter, so I hid in the garden for half a year. Now, we have finally met, and he demands that I pay for my life. I suddenly realized that in a past life, I indeed killed my wife and children. I still remember that after my death, the king of hell judged me for my sins in life, but only the judge had not yet arrived, and I was also punished to be reborn as a woman and become widowed early. All these things are clear in my heart and mind, and it is too late to regret. The three mother and child daily scratch my cheek and choke my throat, not allowing me a moment of peace. The food is not mine, but I do not know when I am full; the calls are not mine, but I cannot stop speaking. Their suffering is very great! Only when my brother is beside me do the three ghosts hide; if others, they do not fear. The reason for enduring silently without speaking is because the matter is too strange and also shameful, but now I cannot help but tell the truth. My brother must spread the story for me in the world, to make people know that cause and effect are clearly manifested, even across lifetimes, they do not forgive. Even now, Buddhist monks' fasting and meditation bring absolutely no benefit. After speaking, tears fell in rows. What is called Zhang Dexin is an old servant of Ye who has been dead for many years.

The deputy magistrate heard it and was greatly alarmed, shouting into the void: "Vengeance begets vengeance, as the law dictates. But if you are indeed wronged, why not seek retribution in a previous life before death, allowing them to live out their natural lives? Why not seek retribution after death, allowing them to be reborn as humans, delaying for more than seventy years? This seems too foolish and unreasonable. Moreover, grudges should be resolved, not held. I will invite a high-ranking monk to help three souls be reborn early. What do you think?" The sister shook her head and said, "My husband said he does not want that, he only needs two pieces of clothing to wear." Leaf immediately prepared three sets of paper clothes, large and small. Just as he was about to enter the room, the sister happily sat up in front of the bed, using both hands to tear and pull, saying, "My wife wore a white cotton shirt, ragged and worn out, completely sewn together with broken threads, unable to undo it. I used all my strength to tear it, finally managing to remove it. Now that I have changed into new clothes, I feel my appearance gradually becoming more human, even if ugly, it looks more human now." In fact, the paper clothes were still on the table and had not been burned, so it was said that the three ghosts had already possessed them.

The prefect shouted again, "Since the clothes have been changed, go quickly!" The sister murmured for a moment and said, "He still wants several ingots of gold and a thousand taels of silver." The prefect looked hesitant, and the sister said, "Do not worry, just a few stems of Buddha grass and a thousand silver ingots." Buddha grass, it turns out, is wheat straw. The family members then gathered wheat straw, recited Buddhist mantras aloud, and cut it. As they did so, a few grains of wheat fell to the ground, and the sister said, "These are excellent pearls, why should we discard them?" They were all instructed to pick them up. In an instant, they collected hundreds of stems of wheat straw. The sister called out, "Stop, they find it too heavy to carry, so give them another bundle." They cut paper into a bundle, along with a thousand silver ingots, and burned them in front of the bed. The sister then closed her eyes and slept soundly, and the prefect went out to meet the guests. After several hours, the sister woke up and asked if the vengeful ghost had left. "Yes, he wanted me to personally see him out of the gate," she replied. "Did the ghost like the clothes?" she was asked. "He did not like them, nor did he thank us, but he said that wearing these clothes would allow him to go out and meet the officials. When I escorted him back through the door, my brother was just seeing Mr. Zheng Six out, and I hid on the side of the door. Didn't he see me?" Mr. Zheng Six, the guest seen by the prefect but unknown to the inner family, caused everyone to be startled. From then on, they slept peacefully and no longer demanded food and drink.

Not three days later, he suddenly cried out, "Here comes the second wife!" Then he called out again, "Here comes the third wife!" In his dream-like speech, he exchanged pleasantries, sometimes laughing and sometimes weeping, talking incessantly. When asked, he said, "These two women are my second and third wives from my previous life. The underworld is holding me accountable for my first wife's affairs and has imprisoned these two women for a long time, preventing them from being reborn. Now, my first wife has obtained my clothes and wealth and has petitioned the various government offices, which have granted permission to release the two women for questioning. That is why they have come to see me first." He also said, "Tomorrow I will be taken to the city god's office for trial; I am doomed!" He sobbed uncontrollably. At the third watch of the night, he cried out in great pain. At dawn, he complained of severe pain in his right thigh. Upon examination, it was swollen and red, as if he had been beaten with a stick. The next day, he complained of pain in his leg, followed by pain in his ankle, all of which were swollen, ulcerated, bleeding profusely, and extremely debilitated. He secretly told the Deputy Magistrate, "My case is无可辩解, and I will admit to everything when I am brought before the authorities. However, I have already been beaten twice and tortured once, yet the case remains unresolved. What can I do?" From then on, he could no longer speak and died after more than ten days. This event occurred in the second month of the year 1760, and the Deputy Magistrate personally recounted it to me.

Muni, a scholar appointed to the ranks of the learned, lived in poverty and cared for his mother. Suddenly, he fell gravely ill and was on the verge of death. Several spirits arrested him and took him to the Eastern Peak. Muni pleaded in sorrow, saying, "My elderly mother is at home, with no one to care for her. If I die, she cannot live alone. Moreover, I have not yet honored my parents or made a name for myself. How can I die immediately? I hope that the Emperor will pity me and grant me more years of life." The Eastern Peak deity replied, "Your fate is limited to being a scholar, and your life span also ends here. The laws of the afterlife are strict, and I cannot accommodate your wishes by increasing your honor, fame, or life span." Muni held onto the desk and cried out in sorrow, and his voice reached the hall and steps. The deity said, "Since you are a disciple of Confucianism, I will send you to the Sage Confucius for judgment." The spirits escorted him to the presence of the Sage. Confucius said, "Life and death belong to the Eastern Peak, and honor and fame belong to Wenchang. I do not participate in this." On his return journey, Muni encountered the Bodhisattva of the Universal Gate, who saw his plea for life and said, "This is a thought of filial piety. Why not grant it to encourage the world?" The spirits replied, "He died several days ago, and his body has rotted. What can we do?" The Bodhisattva commanded the Bodhisattva of Good Talent to go to the Western Heaven to fetch the Muni mud and restore his body. The Bodhisattva of Good Talent went to do so.

Three days later, Shancai wrapped and brought the mud of Muni, which was the color of sandalwood and its fragrance did not dissipate. He went with Shancai to the house, and indeed the body had rotted; flies and gnats sucked on the outside, and worms attacked the inside. He saw a lamp glowing, and the old mother wept. At that time, the death had been seven days, and there was still nothing to bury him. Shancai wrapped the body with the mud three times, and soon the foul smell gradually subsided, the flies and gnats dispersed, and the worms also left. The rotten parts became as good as new, and there was a return of life. Shancai commanded the spirit of Pin to return to it, entering through the mouth, and said, "I return to report to the Bodhisattva." The body immediately wriggled. Pin opened his eyes and saw his mother weeping beside him, and he also wept uncontrollably. The mother was startled and screamed wildly, and neighbors all gathered. Pin had already sat up and said, "Mother, do not be afraid, I have been reborn." So he explained in detail how he met the Bodhisattva and was reborn, saying, "I originally had no merit or fame, and my life span was already over. I sought to repay my parents' kindness. The Bodhisattva commanded me to keep the precepts against greed, lust, meat, and alcohol, and granted me merit, fame, and longevity. I could not abstain from alcohol, but the rest I kept as commanded. The Bodhisattva allowed me to become a Jinshi (imperial scholar), but commanded that I have no official position and not to serve in office." He then looked at his mother and said, "Do not be afraid, I have indeed been reborn." Later, Pin passed the imperial examination in the year of Wu Xu and became the magistrate of Zhending County, where he died in office.

# The Tale of the Otter Spirit Attribution for Source Text ``` ○獭怪郭生者，吴郡名家子，弱冠未娶。一夕读书，有好女子到其家，与之狎。自是过午辄至，不意为生妹窥见，告其父。父疑生有私呢妮因之为婚。 及新妇入房启帐，见好女子在焉，大惊走避，举家哗然。逐之，其女子无惧色，反毅然责生曰：“我与若十年夙姻，奈何恋新婚而逐我耶？”家人求祷于法师施亮生，起醮坛作法，敕王、朱二天君持剑击生。即奔突大呼，良久乃定，瞪目曰：“妖见神将下击，伏我脚下，被神将斫百余创，破颅而遁，殆即死矣。”怪果绝，郭生亦无恙。 ``` 郭生，吴郡名门之后，正值弱冠之年，尚未娶妻。一日读书之时，有一位美丽的女子来到他家中，与他亲近。自此以后，这位女子每日午后都会前来，直到有一天，被郭生的妹妹无意中看到，并告知了她的父亲。父亲怀疑郭生有私情，因此决定为他安排婚姻。 当新妇进入房间掀开帐子时，发现那位美丽的女子也在场，顿时大惊失色，急忙躲藏起来，全家因此而大乱。众人追赶那女子，但她毫无惧色，反而坚定地责备郭生说：“我和你已有十年的深厚情谊，为何因为新婚而驱逐我呢？”家人于是向法师施亮生求救，法师设立醮坛，进行法术，命令王、朱两位天君持剑攻击郭生。郭生顿时狂奔大叫，过了许久才平静下来，瞪着眼睛说：“妖怪见到神将降临，被他们踩在脚下，遭受了上百次的砍击，头颅破裂后逃走，几乎丧命。”果然，怪异之事平息，郭生也安然无恙。 Note: This translation was created by an AI language model. The accuracy of the translation may vary, and it is not intended to be a substitute for a human translation.

Not long after, seven members of the Gu family fell to the ground and died on the same day. Later, they invited a priest to perform rituals, and one of the dead suddenly stood up and cursed, saying, "My ancestor has been alive for a thousand years, and the Gu family killed him. I will destroy the Gu family!" Another person then raised their arm and said, "Do you recognize me as the Upper Official? That woman is a water otter who has lived for a thousand years and has accumulated much merit. She had a karmic connection with the son of the Gu family, and you killed her. Now her descendants are complaining to me, and I have come to avenge them. Your rituals cannot affect me." The priest was still in a state of confusion when suddenly the dead all came back to life. People asked them what had happened, and they replied, "Yesterday, we saw five fierce ghosts who dragged us to a cave. There, we saw a group of monsters carrying a dead otter, with a hundred wounds on its body and a crushed head. The demons mourned with white cords, and those who mourned were all from the category of scaled and shelled creatures. We heard them gather and discuss, planning to rely on a powerful deity for support, offering countless jewels as bribes. The powerful deity is the Upper Official. Greedy for the bribes, he agreed to it. The demons, with the support of the powerful deity, wanted to gather their kind and fight against the priest. Suddenly, we heard the sound of ten thousand horses galloping in the air, and a god in golden armor descended from the sky, dragging dozens or hundreds of iron chains, binding the demons and leaving. That is why we were able to live again." From then on, the Gu family was safe.

The Heavenly Soldier, Zhu Sheng, on the day of his examination, arrived at the examination hall and experienced severe abdominal pain. The examiner, after checking, postponed the exam. Upon returning home, he heard a voice inside his abdomen saying, "I am Yao Zou, from Jinling. In the early Ming Dynasty, I was a subordinate general under the banner of a nobleman from the State of Wei. The nobleman was the third incarnation of Zhu Sheng. Our commander led a thousand men to suppress mountain bandits, but we were surrounded. The commander coveted my wife, Pan, and refused to send reinforcements. I and nearly all of the thousand men were killed or injured, with only a few survivors. Because he forced himself upon my wife, she refused and hanged herself. I have long wished to avenge this, so I have come to demand my life." His family asked, "Why did you not seek revenge at that time, but wait for several hundred years?" The voice replied, "At that time, he was a high-ranking general, loyal and brave, with deep roots of virtue, so I could not avenge myself. In his second incarnation, he became a high monk, and in his third, a prominent official with effective policies, so I still could not avenge myself. Even in this life, he passed the imperial examination, so I could not avenge myself. Now, with just one word, he killed three lives, and his rank and position have been reduced, so I can finally avenge myself." They asked, "What is the matter that killed three lives?" The voice said, "He falsely accused someone on a certain day as a thief, and the person, his wife, and his younger brother all died, is that not killing three lives?" Previously, Zhu Sheng had been robbed, and he suspected that his neighbor Zhang stole it. He reported to the authorities, but due to suspicious behavior and lack of evidence, Zhang, his wife, and his younger brother were implicated and died. This is indeed a fact.

During the same period, there was a man named Zhou in the same town who specialized in dealing with ghosts and monsters, and his methods were quite effective. Upon hearing about Zhu's condition, Zhou went to visit him. Zhu had a fearful expression, and there was no sound coming from his stomach. Zhou left, and then loudly declared, "Am I afraid of you? I am afraid of the *Tianpeng* talisman!" When asked about it, Zhou revealed that he indeed held it in his sleeve. Another wandering monk named Xilian also came to visit Zhu. Seeing Zhu in a state of pain, Xilian began to recite his spell. The voice from Zhu's stomach said, "Virtuous monk, are you reciting a spell to restrain me?" Xilian replied, "I am here to resolve your grudge, why should I restrain you?" The voice from Zhu's stomach responded, "If you want to resolve the grudge, you must recite the *Lotus Sutra*. The spell you hold is the *Huiji Vajra* spell, which orders evil spirits to forcefully restrain me. How can I submit to that?" Xilian said, "I will set up a ritual and recite the *Lotus Sutra*. Can it resolve your grudge and release any long-standing grievances?" The voice from Zhu's stomach agreed, and then requested several pieces of paper money, a formal contract, and a written guarantee. It said, "If you follow my instructions, I will leave. But the important ones will come out through the mouth, and the others will come out through the other orifices." Zhu then vomited several liters of phlegm and urinated for several days, and the sound inside him ceased.

After several days, the stomach spoke again, saying, "My enemy has been resolved, but those surrounded by dead thieves are still many, and they refuse to release, what can be done?" Thus, the sound of thousands of people was heard inside the stomach. Zhu Xing suffered greatly, unable to bear it and died. ○ A doctor from Jiangzhou named Wan Junmo, whose profession was very skilled, had people from far and near coming to seek medical treatment continuously. Wan Junmo treated them all with great care, not considering whether there was any payment. Some poor people were treated at home and sent away after recovery. One day, a Daoist came to the door seeking medical treatment. Wan diagnosed him and said, "Your illness is a blockage in the chest, and taking dozens of doses of medicine can resolve it." The Daoist said, "I come from Mount Lu, what about the round trip?" Wan then kept him and treated him. After a month, he indeed recovered. It was an event at the end of the Chongzhen era. At that time, bandits were rampant, and everywhere feared their sudden arrival. Wan Junmo was worried about it. The Daoist said, "Do you have the strength to move and avoid them?" Wan Junmo said, "Beyond making a living, I have no long-term means of support, and I have no other place to live, what can be done?" Before leaving, the Daoist instructed Wan Junmo to take a bucket of earth and curse it, to store it in the hall of merit, and to burn incense in the morning and evening. If bandits suddenly arrive, take a bowl of earth and scatter it at the front and back doors, close the door and not go out, only eat roasted rice and not cook, wait until the bandits retreat before going out.

The bandits repeatedly attacked the city, and when the soldiers arrived, they all used this method, causing no damage. A neighbor who turned to look remarked, "They only saw clouds and mist." By the time the soil was depleted, the world had already achieved peace. ○ A Novice Monk Reflects on Tigers A Zen master from Mount Wutai took in a novice monk who was just three years old. Mount Wutai stands at its highest point, and the master and disciple practiced atop the mountain without ever descending. After more than ten years, the divine master and disciple descended from the mountain. The novice monk saw cows, horses, chickens, and dogs but did not know what they were. The master then pointed and explained, "This is a cow, used for plowing fields; this is a horse, used for riding; these are chickens and dogs, used for announcing dawn and guarding the gate." The novice monk nodded in agreement. Shortly after, a young woman passed by, and the novice monk was startled and asked, "What is this?" Concerned that the novice might be tempted, the master sternly explained, "This is called a tiger. Anyone who approaches it will surely be bitten to death, leaving no trace of their bones." The novice monk nodded again.

In the evening, when ascending the mountain, the master asked, "Did you think of anything you saw today while you were down in the valley?" The disciple replied, "I did not think of any other things; I only thought of the man-eating tiger, and I always feel unable to let go of it in my heart." ○ Liu Rui, a native of Gu'an village, made a living by selling chickens. At twenty years old, he was quite handsome. One day, driving more than ten chickens to the city to sell, as he approached the city gate, he saw a woman of extraordinary beauty. She called out, "Is it you, Liu Lang? Please sit on the stone, and I have something to say to you. I am a fairy, and I have a connection with you, so I sit here to wait for you. You need not be afraid, I will certainly not harm you, and it will be beneficial to you, but it's a pity that our previous connection only lasts for three years. When you go to sell chickens, you will surely meet someone who buys all of them, and you can empty the basket completely, getting eight thousand four hundred coins." Liu responded obediently, continuing his way, but finally, feeling fear in his heart.

When Liu sold chickens in the city, it turned out just as he had predicted. His heart became even more shocked and suspicious, thinking it was a ghost or spirit, and he thought to avoid it, so he took a detour and returned home by another road. Then the woman was already sitting in his home, smiling and saying, "Our fateful connection was decided long ago. How could you avoid it?" Liu had no choice but to marry her, and she was just like a normal person. By morning, she told Liu, "The house is too small, and I can't get used to living here. You need to build several more rooms." Liu replied, "I only have eight thousand in chicken money. How can I build a house?" The woman said, "You don't need to worry about this. I know that the owner of this house is not your property. Is it the land of your uncle Liu Laizi?" Liu answered, "Yes." She continued, "At this time, Liu Laizi lost two thousand five hundred coins at the gambling place. You should go quickly. He will definitely ask you for silver. You give him the amount, and then you can get the land." Liu went to the gambling place and indeed saw his uncle being tied to a tree by someone who was demanding gambling debts. Seeing Liu Rui, Liu Laizi was overjoyed and said, "If you are willing to pay my gambling debts for me, I am willing to give you the house and land with a written deed." Liu gave him the money, they made the deed, and he returned home. The woman built three more rooms next to the house, which were quite spacious. In an instant, all the household items were there, and nobody knew where they came from.

The neighbors heard about it and came to request a meeting. Liu returned home and asked his daughter, "Can he be allowed?" The daughter replied, "What harm is there in seeing him, but among the neighbors, there is Wang Wu, who has always behaved improperly. I dislike that person, so tell him not to come." Liu informed Wang Wu, but Wang Wu refused, saying, "All the neighbors are coming, why exclude me?" He then rushed in with the group. The neighbors all bowed together, called out to the sister-in-law to ask after her well-being, and the daughter responded with a polite greeting, her expression very gentle. Wang Wu laughed and said, "Sister-in-law, were you comfortable last night?" The daughter cursed and said, "I knew you had accumulated many evils, and I never allowed you to come. How dare you act so wildly!" She shouted sternly, "Tie him up!" Wang Wu was bound with his hands behind his back and made to kneel. She shouted again, "Slap his face!" Wang Wu slapped his cheeks repeatedly. The neighbors then all knelt down and begged for mercy. The daughter said, "For the sake of the other neighbors, kick him out!" Wang Wu stumbled backward and crawled out, fleeing far away afterward and daring not to stay in the village. The daughter bore a son for Liu, who had clear and handsome features, was dignified and reserved in his speech, and Liu's family business became moderately prosperous, and he no longer engaged in chicken trading.

One day, the woman suddenly set out wine, placed her child in Liu's arms, and wept uncontrollably. Liu was startled and asked the reason. She said, "Do you not remember my words from three years ago about the end of our karmic connection? Now it has been three years! Fate determined by heaven, not a single bit off, cannot be extended. But after I leave, you may remarry, and entrust your new wife to raise our child well. Know that I will often come to see him. I can see people, but people cannot see me." She then stood up and left. Liu held onto her clothes and said, "Since you came, our family's fortunes have improved, but now that you are leaving, how will I support myself?" The woman replied, "Your concern is valid, and I have thought about this too." She then took out a wooden puppet from her sleeve, about an inch long, and gave it to Liu, saying, "This person's surname is Zi, and his name is Bu Yu. He is my servant, and he can know past and future events. You should clean and prepare a room to worship him, and you can consult him for all business matters." Liu was surprised and asked, "Is Zi Bu Yu a monster?" She replied, "Yes." Liu asked, "Can a monster be worshipped?" She said, "I am a monster too, so how can you marry me? You must know that all beings are not the same. There are humans who are worse than monsters, and there are monsters who are better than humans. You cannot judge them by one standard. But this puppet is very ugly, so I named him Zi Bu Yu, and he does not want to be seen by people. Just worship him in the room and listen to his sounds."

Liu Congzhi placed a puppet in a tower and offered incense and candles. When he called out to "Mother Who Does Not Speak," it responded as clearly as an echo. The entire family heard the voice but could not see its form. Wine and food were sent up to the tower, and all the dishes were empty, but only the sound of eating could be heard. The footprints on the ladder were very small, indicating the small size of the shoes. When the woman was leaving, she still slept with Liu for three days and nights. In the morning, when he touched her, she was gone, the window was closed, and it was unknown where she went. For three years, they offered to Mother Who Does Not Speak, and she answered all questions and all plans were beneficial. Suddenly, one day, this woman returned from the sky and took Liu's hand, saying, "Does your family have three thousand taels of silver?" He replied, "Yes." She said, "If so, your fortune is sufficient. Not only will I leave, but I will also take Mother Who Does Not Speak with me." Afterward, when calling to the tower, no one answered. His son named Zhao entered the county school, and Marshal Hua Tengxiao personally saw him. ○ A Skeleton's Self-Praise In Suzhou, there was a Buddhist temple on the Upper Mountain. A man surnamed Wang from Yangzhou stayed at the temple. During the day, he heard murmuring voices from the steps below. He summoned other guests to listen, and all heard them. They suspected that there was a ghost complaining of injustice, so they gathered the monks and used plows and shovels to dig. They dug about five feet deep and found a rotten coffin containing a single skeleton, with nothing else inside. They then covered it back up as before. --- This translation aims to capture the essence and nuance of the original text while ensuring it reads naturally in English.

Not even half a moment had passed when a low, indistinct voice was heard from the ground, as if it were coming from within the coffin. Everyone leaned forward to listen, but they could not make out a single word, and they all became alarmed and suspicious. Someone said, "There is a monk named De Yin in the west wing, who has very high moral character and can communicate with ghosts. Let us invite him to listen." Wang immediately invited the monk to come. The monk bent down and said after a long pause, "Do not pay attention to it. This ghost was a high-ranking official in his past life, well-liked and flattered by others, but after death, no one flatters him anymore. Therefore, he constantly praises himself in the coffin." The crowd laughed and dispersed, and the sound from the ground gradually faded away. ○ A Wisteria Flower Sends Off the End In the Ministry of Personnel, there was a wisteria flower, a thousand-year-old plant with an ancient trunk as large as a dragon, so massive that one person could not encircle it with their arms. Its leaves covered three rooms, making it especially cool in summer, and it bloomed alongside peonies. In the sixth year of the Qianlong era, the prime minister Gan Gong Rui and the brave general Na Qin were selecting officials in the hall. Just as names were being called and lots drawn, Gan Gong Rui died in his chair, his hand still holding the pen but not falling. Na Gong reported this to the emperor, who rewarded him with a thousand taels of silver and ordered the management of his funeral. That evening, the wisteria flower bloomed abundantly, with buds and flowers fragrant for three days, ten times more abundant than in late spring. It is unknown what sign this is.

In Volume 30, the magistrate of Changzhou, Jiang Mingfu, says: The lion and elephant that the Buddha rides are well-known to people; the *kong* (犭孔) that the Buddha rides is not known to people, and the *kong* is a transformation of a zombie. There was a certain person who was traveling at night and saw a corpse emerging from its coffin. Knowing that it was a zombie, he waited for it to come out, then took tiles and stones to fill the coffin, and climbed up to the upper floor of a farmhouse to watch. When it was close to the fourth watch of the night, the zombie came back with large strides, as if holding something in its hands. When it reached the coffin, it could not enter, and it opened its eyes wide, glaring angrily, with its light shining brightly. Seeing someone on the upper floor, the zombie came to seek them out. Its legs were hard as dry wood and it could not climb the ladder, so it became angry and removed the ladder. Fearing that he could not get down, the person climbed down a tree branch and fell. The zombie realized this and chased after him. Fortunately, he was skilled in swimming and thought that the zombie could not enter the water, so he swam across and stood on the other side. It made strange sounds and mournful cries, leaped three times, and jumped three times, then transformed into an animal form and left. On the ground, there was a child's body, which had been partially chewed, leaving only half of it, and the blood had completely dried up.

It is said that a corpse first transforms into a drought spirit (旱魃), then into a mythical creature (犭孔). The 犭孔 has supernatural powers, breathing fire from its mouth and capable of fighting dragons, hence the Buddha rides it to subdue it. In the 27th year of the Qianlong Emperor's reign, a merchant named Ye from Hangzhou built a garden and dug a pond, during which he discovered two large jars, their tops and bottoms joined together. Suspecting there was a hidden treasure, he ordered someone to open them, and inside found a Daoist sitting cross-legged. The Daoist had claws about a zhang long (approximately 3 meters), circling his body three times, with wide-open eyes, half-smiling and half-not smiling. When asked, "Of what dynasty is this person?" the Daoist shook his head without answering. The merchant gave him tea and soup, but he could not speak. The merchant, being a wealthy man who liked to do good deeds, gave him ginseng soup to drink, but he still could not speak, only smiling. The merchant thought it was a Daoist immortal who had not yet completed his transformation, and intended to cover and hide it as before. The merchant's servant, Xi'er, wanted to take its claws to show off as a strange object, secretly took and cut them. Mistakenly injuring its body, blood flowed out. The Daoist wept with tears, then immediately died, turning into a pile of dry bones.

I have examined the *Nanshi* (*Southern History*) biographies, which record that someone dug up a grave and opened a coffin, discovering a woman who claimed she would soon become an earth deity and warned them to be careful not to harm her. The grave-digger, greedy for her golden bracelet, cut off her wrist to take it, and her blood flowed as she turned into dry bones. Only then did I realize that the same things happen in ancient and modern times, it is probably fate or destiny; the story is recorded in the biography of Wang Yuanmo. --- Zhang the King of Hell There was a scholar named Zhang in Hangzhou who was always of bad conduct and domineering in his village. One day, while visiting a friend's home, he heard that there was a female witch in a certain village who could summon ghosts and spirits, and she had many followers. Zhang went to see her, and at that time, the witch was performing a ritual, with onlookers gathered like a wall. Zhang stepped forward and slapped her cheek, saying, "You are deceiving the people with your false words; you cannot escape punishment. If I were the King of Hell, I would definitely cut you down." The onlookers left one by one. Shortly after, the witch indeed fell ill with a scalp abscess and died. People then called him 'Zhang the King of Hell.'

After several more years, Zhang fell ill and met two officials whom he had never seen before. They invited him to accompany them. They went to a government office where the grand hall was magnificent, and two gods sat on either side with their curtains drawn. In front of the central god's throne hung a curtain, and the face was not visible. Zhang asked, "Why did the gods summon me?" The gods replied, "A female witch reported to you, so we summoned you to question you. Your judgment of his crime was very appropriate, and there was no injustice, but you are also not a righteous person. You must list all your sins before death and confess them one by one." The attendants were ordered to give him a tablet, and he wrote on it himself. After writing on both sides, he still felt it was not enough. The god looked at it and said, "These few cases are already sufficient. What do you think you deserve?" Zhang thought for a long time and said, "Should be struck by lightning." The god said, "Not enough to cover the guilt. Should be struck three times." The god ordered the attendants to lift the curtain in the hall and told Zhang to look up. Clearly already a likeness, he finally realized that his previous life was as the King of Hell. Because of his sins, he was reborn into the human world. Soon, the two officials came again to send Zhang back to his hometown. As if waking from a dream, drenched in sweat. From then on, he reformed and did good, completely erasing his past mistakes.

One day, a fierce storm with lightning and thunder struck Zhang down, and he was dead on the ground. Shortly afterward, he revived. Another few months later, while watching a play on the stage, the lightning struck again, and Zhang knew he was being targeted. He shouted for everyone to take cover, but he was indeed struck dead. A short while later, he revived again, staggering back home, where he resumed teaching children in the village. On another day, the thunder rumbled loudly, circling the house without stopping. Zhang feared that if struck a third time, he might not survive, so he hid himself under a black lacquered table. A clap of thunder sounded, burning down the bed and curtains, but Zhang was miraculously spared. Knowing his fate was over, he continued to prepare for the imperial examinations. Two years later, he was selected as a filial and upright official. However, he did not pass the metropolitan examination and accompanied his relative, Cai Jieping, the governor of Hunan, to take up his post. Passing through Hanyang, he heard that a certain fortune-teller was extremely accurate, so he went to visit him. The fortune-teller said, "You will have a small good fortune this time, but your life is over. You can only stay for one year before returning, and do not linger. When you return, come to see me again; I have important matters to entrust to you." Zhang thought about his words and returned on schedule. He went to visit the fortune-teller again, but the man was already dead, leaving a letter. Zhang opened and read it, and it was a request for him to bring his coffin home. Zhang loaded the coffin and returned to Hangzhou. Not even a month later, he died peacefully at home.

I have examined *Guang Bówù Zhì* and it states, "Where lightning strikes, even metal and stone are consumed, only lacquerware remains unharmed." Zhang was saved on the third attempt, perhaps this is the reason? ○ A woman from the Liang family, newly married, was visited by Zhang, a scholar from Hangzhou. He said that the newly married woman had been married for only a few days when she suddenly became mute, speaking in northern dialect, and babbling incoherently. Upon closer inspection, it was found that she was speaking in the manner of her deceased brother. Her brother was the son of Yao Hetai and served as a local official in Guangxi province, where he died in office. She addressed the new wife as her sister and said, "There is an important matter, please let the master speak to me in person." The master happened to have a foot ailment and could not climb the stairs, so he asked his wife to go up instead. The new wife said, "I have nothing else to say, I just want to build a temple for the Mother of the Stars, and then I will leave." The wife refused and said, "If you want to build a temple for the stars, it is the business of the Yao family, not the Liang family." She then said, "My sister and I were both attendants of the Mother of the Stars in our previous lives. Now the Yao family is poor and unable to afford it, so it must be the Liang family. If you do not agree, I will go back with my sister to our previous positions." The wife had no choice but to agree. The new wife said, "Unless you swear an oath, I will not believe you." Then the family all thought it was unacceptable and argued with her for a long time. Yao's son was not a follower of Buddhism or Daoism in his lifetime, but after his death, he suddenly wanted to venerate the stars, which is very puzzling.

Hangzhou Tale: A Newlywed Woman Carrying a Treasure Bottle A newlywed woman, holding a treasure bottle filled with five grains, performed the ritual of alternating actions as she entered her new home. Liang, the new bride, carried the bottle through the city gate when the gatekeeper demanded money and caused a disturbance. The bride was greatly startled and subsequently felt disoriented. After drinking magical water, her spirit and soul became somewhat settled, and she said, "I have three souls: one is lost at the city gate, and one is lost in the bottle; I must retrieve them from both places." Her family followed her instructions. The bride then said, "The soul at the city gate has returned, but the soul in the bottle is trapped under a rice cabinet and cannot be freed. What shall I do?" This is a Hangzhou custom where the bride's bottle is placed in a rice cabinet. Following her instructions, her illness improved, but she remained disoriented.

A Small Maid Enters a Hole Another account states: His respected elder, Mr. Xingzi, who was the supervisor of education in Jiangxi, had a small maid who was very dull but suddenly became clever, which surprised the family. One day, she closed the door and went to bathe, but after a long time, she did not come out. When they called her, there was no response. They peered inside and found no one. They pried open the door and entered, and they found the water in the bathtub still warm. All the windows were closed, and not a speck of dust moved. However, there was a small hole in the floor, only large enough for a mouse to enter and exit. They opened the board and searched, and found a hole deep about a zhang. The maid lay in it, dazed and unconscious. They poured ginger juice on her, and after a long time, she woke up. She said, "A month ago, I met a young woman who treated me very kindly and taught me diligently. I suddenly changed from being dull to being clever, all because of this woman's teaching. She told me, 'I have a grievance, and I want your master to clear it up.' I promised her, but I was too afraid to tell my master. After several days, the woman came and scolded me for breaking my promise. I told her I was afraid of my master, so I didn't dare. The woman said, 'What you said also makes sense, I won't blame you. I have a beautiful garden, why don't you come with me to visit it?' So she pulled me to a place, where there was a small red door and a few narrow rooms. I said, 'There was nothing to see, I want to go back.' The woman said, 'Let's sit down for a while, rest your legs.' Suddenly, they heard a commotion outside, and the woman was startled and ran away. I realized you came to find me." So she pulled me out of the hole, and the ghost disappeared.

A servant girl, when she was sixteen or seventeen, was married off immediately and has remained safe and sound until she is over fifty. ○ The spirit of a wrongfully dead soul, named Wang, from Yanzhou, a scholar who died while taking the provincial examination at Qililong, was guided by a medium named Yuan Yuliang from Cixi. Wang reported that those who die by drowning are initially collected by someone and taken to a place similar to a modern office, where an official named Si Guan is in charge. The next day, their place of origin is checked, and officials are sent to take them to the king of hell. When they set out, they are sent off with a blast of the copper dragon. The copper dragon is made of copper, has a curved handle, and sounds very sad and poignant, like a small trumpet on a horse. When Wang arrived in the underworld, the king checked his life and found no major evil deeds, so he was released, not allowed to be reborn, and not restrained by anyone, allowing him to wander freely, hence he came here. He also said that ghosts have no fun, always suffer from cold, and must approach human bodies to absorb their vital energy to feel comfortable. If several ghosts compete to absorb energy at the same time, one mistake can bring them close to a human body, causing a burning sensation.

The ghost also fears strong winds. When the wind rises, it must lie down and cannot move, for strong winds carry a supernatural force that, when it hits the ghost's body, makes it as heavy as a mountain. Every time the wind rises, it looks as black as ink. When encountering strong winds, it feels like passing pieces of wood or paper over the ghost's back, which can make the ghost's body dissipate. Moreover, the ghost suffers from hunger and frequently enters people's homes to steal the aroma of food as sustenance. In wealthy households where fat is abundant, the aroma of food is rich, and eating it can withstand hunger; in poor households, the aroma of food is weak and insufficient for a full meal. When stealing food, there is often a child guarding the pot, as the child is under the jurisdiction of the Kitchen God. Every time a ghost steals the aroma of food, the child will chase after it, making it difficult to obtain food from wealthy households. To steal the aroma of food, one must wait until the food is cooked and the pot is opened. If there is wind, the aroma of food disperses, and the ghost grabs it with their hands, like silk or cotton, and can eat it. If there is no wind, the aroma of food rises and is guarded by the child, making it impossible to steal.

Breaking the Ghost's Example One day, while Li was reading at night, his house was near the water. He heard a ghost say, "Tomorrow, someone will come to cross the water, and that person will be my substitute." On the next day, indeed, someone came to cross the water. Li tried to stop him, but the person did not cross and left. At night, the ghost came to blame him, saying, "What have you done to me that I cannot get a substitute?" Li asked, "Why do you need a substitute for your cycle of rebirth?" The ghost replied, "In the underworld, it has always been like this. I don't know where it began. It's similar to how in the human world, one must wait for a vacancy before filling a position or grain ration." Li explained to him, "You are mistaken! The granaries have grain, and officials have salaries, all of which are state resources. They cannot be wasted, so there are limits. In the human world, people live and die naturally, and they sustain themselves with their own efforts. The natural order does not have time to manage such trivial matters." The ghost said, "I heard that the Wheel-Turning King actually manages this account." Li said, "Then you should take this message to the Wheel-Turning King. If he thinks a substitute is necessary, you should come and drag me to be your substitute, so I can see the Wheel-Turning King and curse him to his face." The ghost was greatly pleased, jumped away, and never came back again.

A Taoist Left a Talisman for Wu of Changzhou Wu, the ancestor of the Ministry of Punishment's Langzhong named Ji, had always been fond of Taoism. After returning from the capital, he met a Taoist in a tavern who had an extraordinary appearance and demeanor, traveling without any luggage. At night, Wu secretly observed the Taoist, who was sitting naked with breath emanating heavily from his ears, such that flies dared not approach. In the morning, as the Taoist was about to depart, Wu asked where he was going. The Taoist replied, "I wander aimlessly without a fixed place." Wu persuaded him to return south, treating him with great respect. They lived together for several years, and on his deathbed, the Taoist gave Wu two talismans, saying, "I have not yet repaid your kindness. If there is trouble in the future, you can use these talismans to subdue or control, as a way to thank you." Later, Wu passed away, and his wife fell seriously ill and was on the verge of death, frequently seeing ghosts and spirits. She sent a maid to watch over her at night. There was a servant who was usually strong and healthy, enjoyed drinking, and had courage. He set up a table outside and had already drunk and fallen asleep. In his sleep, he dreamed of an old man accompanied by a young boy, each holding a pot and a cup. The old man said to the young boy, "He enjoys drinking, so let him have a cup." The young boy placed a cup inside the old servant's navel and poured it. Initially, the servant felt very hot, but later could not bear it, and then shouted loudly and woke up, coughing once, with blood spurting out and covering the ground. From then on, the ghosts became even more rampant.

A few days later, the family tidied up the place to prepare for the lady's coffin. They accidentally found a Taoist talisman in a box and nailed it to the canopy. The lady, who had been silent for a long time, suddenly exclaimed, "There is a bright mirror hanging on the canopy, with an armored general holding a sword chasing away ghosts, and all the ghosts have fled far away." From then on, her illness was cured, and she passed away after another ten years. Friends and relatives with illnesses borrowed the talisman to drive away ghosts, and all were effective. However, it eventually disappeared. ○ The Top Scholar Must Sacrifice His Lifespan During the reign of Emperor Kangxi in the year 1683, scholars from the Jiangnan region traveled to the capital for the imperial examination. One top scholar, arrogant and belittling his peers, boasted, "This year's top scholar, if not me, who else?" His peers could not bear his insults. When they arrived in the capital, the exam was approaching, and one of the fellow scholars dreamed that the God of Literature ascended the hall and announced the results. When the names were called, the top scholar was indeed named as the top scholar. The fellow scholar felt secretly resentful. Soon after, a woman with disheveled hair cried out in distress, "This person's conduct is flawed; he should not be crowned the top scholar among the candidates. We must replace him with someone else." The God of Literature looked troubled and turned to the God of Literature's attendant for advice. The attendant replied, "During the reign of Emperor Wanli, there was a similar incident. The top scholar of the next year was moved to the current year. That person passed the exam three years earlier but lost six years of his life. This precedent can be followed today." Thus, the names were called again, and the top scholar became Wang Shidan.

At dawn, a certain person spoke boastfully as usual, but fellow students told him about a dream they had. The person paled and said, "This is an inescapable karmic debt." Not only did he stop thinking about becoming the top scholar, but he also ceased taking the examination altogether. He hastily packed his belongings and returned home, dying halfway. That year's top scholar was indeed Wang Shidan, who lived to be sixty years old. ○ According to Qiantang's Qian Yinting, he once rented a boat from Tianjin to return to Hangzhou. On the same boat was a man surnamed Yang, a scholar from Wuxi, who sat quietly in the boat, rarely speaking. Qian, because of Yang's reserved nature, did not engage in conversation with him either. One day, Yang mentioned the concept of cause and effect, and Qian did not believe it. Yang, however, strongly affirmed its existence and said that within a month, he had several nights of official duty in the underworld, specifically responsible for fetching human lives. All were recorded on a single ticket with the person's name written on it. If the person had a life of honor, including princes, kings, generals, and ministers, a red seal would be added, similar to an official document in the human world. The seal's text resembled the seal script of official seals, but he did not recognize what character it was. Yama, the king of the underworld, inquired about good and evil in the human world, first using a robe to cover the person's body, like a bell in the human world. When wearing this robe, ambiguous and guilty deeds would unconsciously be confessed. The underworld treated people very leniently; if a person had a bad thought in the human world but also had a good thought, the previous bad thought would be canceled out. The person in charge of this seal was Yu Zongxu, a high-ranking official during the Ming dynasty, who has not been moved yet.

A Man from the Demon Country Returns to Lin'an A man from the demon country, Zhao Yiji, returned to Lin'an (modern-day Hangzhou). He met a monk who told him that on February 6th of this year, two men from Lin'an, one named Zhao and the other named Li, had come to sell pigs in Hangzhou. Halfway there, Zhao had already sold his pigs and wanted to go home first. Li wanted to go home together, but Zhao refused, and Li angrily scolded him, saying, "Even if you go, there will definitely be evil spirits blocking your way, and you won't be able to get home." Zhao disliked what he said and prayed at the Xuantan Temple before continuing his journey. When he reached a large bridge, it was already the second watch of the night, and he indeed saw four people ahead: they had messy hair and frightening faces, with blood flowing from all seven orifices, and they surrounded him in a circle. Zhao, relying on his bravery, wanted to punch one of them, but a ghost directly put a black cloth over his head. He immediately felt a cold sensation attacking his heart, became unable to speak, and fell to the ground. The group of ghosts blocked his mouth and nose with mud. Suddenly, someone appeared holding a stick and drove away the four ghosts. The person lifted Zhao by hand and threw him, saying, "I came to save you. I am the Xuantan God. These four ghosts fled into China to cause trouble because last year, the demon country was in a state of famine, and hungry ghosts had nowhere to find food. What you encountered is a demon's iron ghost. But you have escaped this disaster, but there may be future troubles. You must use thirteen sticks of incense, from the stove to the front door, only then can you be completely safe."

Zhao awoke, not expecting to find himself lying outside his own door, and he looked up to the sky and bowed in gratitude. As he had said, he was indeed safe and sound. In Shaoxing, there was a man named Li Zhiying, who served as an official in Taiguxian, Shandong Province. One night, while sleeping in his study, an old man extended his shoes under the bed and said, "I am a person from Shanyin, also a guest official. I died and could not return home, and the servant stole my silver, letters, and clothes and fled. Until now, the family still does not know. I ask you to send a letter home for me." Li replied, "There is no need to send a letter. I will return home soon, and when I return, I will send your coffin back." The ghost was very happy and bowed to thank him, and said, "There is nothing to repay your kindness. I wish to help you solve cases." From then on, every night Li slept soundly, and the cases on his desk were already solved. He was once called a divine being. After a year, he sent the coffin back, and his wife and children wept and welcomed him at the door, saying, "Last night I dreamed that the old gentleman's spirit returned home, so I am here to welcome you." A candidate for the imperial examinations from Bozhou named Gao, whose family was quite wealthy, lived five miles west of the city, in a place called Xiaozhen. His family had many powerful servants, all relying on the master's power, who acted arrogantly in the countryside. A local farmer named Chen had several acres of land, near the Gao family's residence. His crops were repeatedly trampled by Gao's horses and donkeys. He tried to reason with them, but was instead insulted by the powerful servant. Chen realized he could not compete with them and no one dared to do anything about it. He fell ill with a chest disease and was on the verge of death after a year.

One day, Old Chen called the workers to his house to make a coffin and told them, "After the coffin, open a small hole for me." Those who heard all exclaimed and asked the reason. Old Chen said, "I was bullied by Gao, and died of anger. I believe I cannot avenge myself while alive, so I want to turn into a snake after death to eat Gao's heart and liver, to vent my hatred." The workers laughed and followed his request. By evening, the craftsmen returned past Gao's house, all considering this as news and laughed loudly. Just happened to be Gao standing idly outside, saw the crowd laughing wildly, and because there were some who were familiar with him, he asked them, and they told him what Old Chen said. Gao was startled and said, "I really didn't know." The next morning, he went to Old Chen's house and said, "The previous events were all due to the family's recklessness, so I came personally to apologize, hope you forgive me." Old Chen said, "You really didn't know, can you scold the family members in front of me, then I won't hate you." Gao said, "Yes." Then he invited Old Chen to his house, scolded the family members severely, and made them bow and apologize, and invited him for a small drink. Old Chen was very pleased and could eat and drink. Suddenly, he felt nauseous and vomited out something about a foot long. The crowd looked at it, and it was a small snake, swimming in phlegm. Gao was startled and said, "If I hadn't apologized today, then you would have turned into a snake to avenge yourself," and afterwards, Old Chen's illness also healed.

**Heart Sutra to Exorcise Foxes** Zheng Guoxiang, a young scholar from Qiantang, had a sister who married into the Luo family. During the early days of October in the year 1694 (the first year of the Jia Shen period of the Kangxi era), while she was sitting by the window at night, a sudden gust of wind entered through the gap in the window, carrying a faint presence. Soon after, a young, well-dressed woman appeared, laughing and coming closer, followed by a small, furry creature, no more than three feet tall, wearing a half-sleeved garment. The woman spoke and laughed with Zheng's sister, and without realizing it, the sister followed her, moving through both forests and cities, their movements swift and effortless. The sister did not realize that her soul had left her body. Sometimes, she would remain motionless for three to five days before waking up. The demon warned her not to reveal this, stating that doing so would harm her life, so she dared not tell anyone. Her family believed that she was acting madly. In the year 1695 (the second year of the Yi You period), Zheng Guoxiang went far away to take the provincial examination and brought his sister home. On the evening of the Mid-Autumn Festival, he questioned her repeatedly, and she finally revealed the truth. That night, the demon caused trouble until the fifth watch of the night and then left. The next night, it returned, and the sister fainted. Zheng grabbed his sister's collar and recited the Heart Sutra, which finally released her and allowed her to return. Every day, he prayed devoutly before the Bodhisattva, wishing to print and distribute over two thousand copies of the Heart Sutra to drive away the demon and save his sister. On that night, when the demon returned, the whole family recited the Bodhisattva's name. After a short while, the sister woke up and said, "When I was in great danger, a Bodhisattva appeared in the air and called out, 'Wicked beast, how dare you come here?' The demon replied, 'I was just looking for food.' The Bodhisattva scolded it, and it left, pointing a finger at the demon, causing it to fly up into the air. The demon disappeared." Everyone smelled the fragrance of sandalwood filling the room, and the sister could sleep peacefully.

The next day in the afternoon, a female spirit suddenly possessed her, and her voice sounded northern. The official took the *I Ching* to subdue her, but she said, "‘The Creative: Great indeed is the Creative; it is advantageous to undertake great deeds; it is correct to be firm,’ I have read it before, so there is no need to take it." Her mouth only called out, "Return my Brother Hu San to me," without ceasing. The official then asked her one by one, and she said, "My surname is Miao, and my name is Miao San Gu. At the age of sixteen, I was picking lotus flowers by the pond when I saw a beautiful woman who smiled and spoke to me, saying she was Wang Da Gu. Behind her was Brother Hu San, whose name was Jiang Heng, and he called himself the third among the great foxes of the world, hence Brother Hu San. I was bewitched by him, and thus I died. Wang Da Gu managed to escape and was reborn, and it has been forty-two years since then. I relied on Brother Hu San to find a replacement. Last October, together with your sister, I found three candidates, expecting that within a year, one of them would be fully possessed, and could then replace me. Now that Brother Hu San has been taken, I have nowhere to return to. What can be done?" The official asked, "Why don't you return to your mother's or husband's family?" She replied, "My mother's family is far in Jiangxi, and I cannot go. In July, I saw my husband snatch food at the Lantern Festival, and I suspect he is no longer in the world of the living." After saying this, she was filled with sorrow.

The national minister performed a Buddhist ritual to liberate the deceased by reciting the *Heart Sutra* three hundred scrolls. Immediately, the deceased clasped her hands in prayer and bowed, saying, "If this is true, I can be reborn in the human world. How about you recite two scrolls for me first?" For each scroll the minister recited, "缪" would recite "Amitabha Buddha" once. After reciting three or four scrolls, the minister said, "There is no need to recite more; if there is more, it would be too heavy, and I cannot hold it in my hands." The deceased then requested fire alcohol, beef, five hundred silver ingots, a tobacco pipe, and a pouch, agreeing to all of them, stood up, performed a bow, thanked, and left. After a meal, the sister's illness began to improve, and she made moaning sounds, saying, "I was hidden in a mountain cave by '缪三姑' and was crying. Suddenly, I saw '缪三姑' with a slightly reddened face, as if she had been drinking alcohol, with silver ingots in her bosom, reciting the tobacco pipe, and holding a white paper sutra scroll, claiming to come from 'Shipra' (likely a misinterpretation of 'Sarvaprajñāpāramitā'). She said, 'Your parents and brothers are thinking of you and will bring you back.' I walked with sore feet, so I moaned."

The next morning, she suddenly spoke incoherently, saying, "The Bodhisattva does not wish to kill Hu San Ge; he merely confines him. Now I have heard that Hu San Ge intends to dig a hole a thousand feet deep to escape, which would harm your sister's life. I am grateful for your kindness, so I come to inform you. Master, you can once again pray to the Bodhisattva to prevent him from escaping." The national minister then devoutly prayed before the Bodhisattva, vowing to print and distribute another 1,000 copies of the Heart Sutra, making a total of 3,000 copies, and to include the matter of Hu San Ge becoming a demon in the text afterward, to widely encourage the world. After the prayer, Mu San Nu said, "This is very good, but the silver ingots I received yesterday were insufficient." She also said, "The Heart Sutra was snatched and torn apart by someone, and the pipe was lost due to being startled by a dog's barking. Now I need a thousand silver ingots, two pairs of skirts, and still require the pipe, pouch, distilled liquor, and beef. Grant me the Heart Sutra, and I can first recite 30 volumes. You must make a paper box, open it, and recite facing the box, and the number of volumes will naturally be included." She also said, "On the first day of the ninth month, you can make offerings to the Bodhisattva, dedicate your sister to the Bodhisattva, and name her Guan Zhen. Make an silver lock, engrave the dharma name on it, and hang it on her chest to avoid misfortune and ensure longevity." Thus, all the items were prepared and delivered at evening. She also said, "At this moment, the Bodhisattva has taken Hu San Ge to the City God's place, and your sister has also gone for trial."

After dusk, my sister Su said, "The City God Temple held a trial, and upon returning, I will recount it. First, outside the temple gate, I saw the City God receive the Bodhisattva to ascend the main hall and sit upright, while the City God sat to the side and slightly lower. I knelt beside the Bodhisattva, and Hu San Ge knelt at the base of the hall. The Bodhisattva spoke to the City God, who then addressed Hu San Ge: 'Wicked creature, how dare you disturb living beings?' Hu San Ge replied, 'I originally lived in the New Official Bridge, but due to its demolition and reconstruction, I borrowed residence in Luo's empty building. This is a female ghost, and she follows me to find food.' The City God immediately ordered the Judge to investigate the records of my parents and my brother, as well as those of the Luo household. Upon completion, the City God shouted, 'He is a living being, how can he be called a female ghost!' He ordered a slap on the face. After the slap, he drew a lot and threw it on the ground, then severely beat Hu San Ge thirty times, saying, 'I will not further investigate you here, but will take you to the True Man's Office to be punished.' He then summoned two officials, prepared the documents, and took Hu San Ge away. The executioner held a red stick and locked and took Hu San Ge away. The Bodhisattva ascended to heaven from the temple, and I also left the temple gate, where Miao San Gu led me back." Thus, a shaman was invited to perform a memorial service for Miao San Gu, and they departed together, never to return.

On the 26th night, her sister had a dream at midnight in which two officials appeared. One of them held a long spear with a head-like object attached, which had bloodstains. The official said, "Hu San has been executed." The sister was startled awake. The next night, as she was just about to lie down, a head-like object rolled to the ground and bit her left arm through her clothes, leaving a painful mark. She immediately cried out, but the head was not seen; only the bloodstain on her left arm remained. From then on, either during the day or at night, she would see the head-like object rolling around near her feet. On the first day of the ninth month, following the advice of Mu San's aunt, they set up locks and carved names, and offered food and drink to the Great Bodhisattva. The sister saw the Great Bodhisattva give instructions: "Hu San has already been executed. You must never go southeast in your lifetime. Your brother promised Mu San's aunt 300 volumes of the Heart Sutra. He has received this sutra and has become a terrestrial immortal. My Heart Sutra is significant, and your brother must show it proper respect." The Great Bodhisattva then took incense ash and wrote a charm on the girl's head to calm her, and she woke up. The brothers and sisters of the country knelt to thank the Great Bodhisattva. However, the head-like object would occasionally come to disturb, and the brothers and sisters would also dream of fighting with someone, though they could not see the form of the opponent, only feeling a dark object no more than three feet long. Suddenly, they realized that the power of the Heart Sutra is vast and can resolve grudges, knots, and liberate suffering souls. They bowed again before the Great Bodhisattva and vowed to recite 300 volumes of the Heart Sutra to liberate Hu San's soul and resolve this matter. From then on, the head-like object was never seen again. These are all things that the brothers and sisters of the country have personally experienced, and they shared them with others.

○ There are three types of *huanba*: one resembling an animal, one transformed from a zombie, and all can cause droughts and stop rain. Only the supreme *huanba*, known as *shangshang huanba*, is particularly destructive, being human-like but with a long head and a single eye on top of the head. It can devour dragons and is feared by rain deities. When it sees clouds rising, it looks up and blows, causing the clouds to disperse and the sun to become more intense, a power beyond human control. Some say that when the heavens decree a drought, the *huanba* forms from the fusion of mountain and river spirits. If it suddenly disappears, it will rain. ○ The feet of ghosts are very fragrant. Mr. Zhou, a scholar from Ningbo, was working in the office in Yuqian. After a long time, he became thin and weak. His colleagues suspected something and asked him, but he always said there was nothing wrong. One day, while eating watermelon together, a guest said that ghosts have no feet. Suddenly, Mr. Zhou said, "Ghosts not only have feet, but the feet of female ghosts are very fragrant." Everyone asked, "What evidence do you have?" Mr. Zhou regretted saying too much. Under persistent questioning, he finally admitted that one night, under the moonlight, he felt a touch and sighed to himself. He saw a beautiful woman across the corridor also sighing to the moon. Mr. Zhou initially thought she was someone from the office and was not afraid. He asked her why she was sighing, and she replied, "You don't know why I am sighing, just as I don't know why you are sighing." Soon after, Mr. Zhou closed the door and went to bed, regretting meeting this beautiful woman at night and wishing he had talked more. Suddenly, he heard a small voice outside the window saying, "If you really want to meet, we can meet again under the moonlight tomorrow night."

By the next night, Zhou secluded his servants and waited for each other under the moon. After a long time, he suspected that she had broken the agreement. By the fourth watch of the night, he suddenly saw a woman stumbling and coming, saying, "I have rushed a thousand miles for you." He asked the reason, and she replied, "Tonight, I am going to Jiangnan's Liuhe to celebrate my sister's birthday. When I left, there were several companions. Afraid of staying too long and breaking the agreement, I left my companions and returned alone. On the way, I feared encountering tigers and wolves, so I was afraid and walked slowly, thus being late. The sky is gradually becoming light, and I cannot linger. If you must meet, can live separately from the servants, afraid of offending the yin and yang." As he said, Zhou followed her advice. The servant knew there was a ghost in the master's room and stuck and refused to move. Zhou became very angry, and the servant finally followed, but every night he would peek into the master's room. The woman no longer came, and after a long time, the servant also became indifferent and no longer disturbed.

A woman suddenly appeared and said, "Do not be afraid, I am the concubine of a former retainer of a certain person from Songjiang. I had a minor illness and was misled by a mediocre doctor, so I died. Because my earthly life was not yet complete, I was not recorded in the underworld's records, and I can roam freely. I checked the *Book of Fleeting Marriages* and found that I have a connection with you, but it is fated that we should have intercourse only 116 times. If no one knows about it, we can stay together longer; otherwise, the connection will end and we will separate." She also said, "You have another person who also has a predestined connection, destined for hundreds of times, but it is unknown when they will meet. From now on, you can become a terrestrial immortal and no longer reincarnate. My menstruation and pregnancy are the same as others, but you are destined to have no children, so I cannot continue your lineage." From then on, Zhou's physical and spiritual state became increasingly exhausted. The group knew about this and urged him to return. Zhou, because everyone knew, could not feel at ease and returned to Ningbo, where his body gradually regained strength. Zhou had intercourse with the woman every time, using medicine to mark a circle on the calendar for each encounter. The group counted the circles, and they reached 116. ○ Wang Bi, Wang Bi, courtesy name Liangfu, was from Qin Prefecture. He practiced medicine in Yan'an and encountered the witch Wang Wanli and his grandson Shangxian, who were fortune-telling in Longsha.
